No. Notified Amount (₹ crore) Tenor (day) Window Timing Date of Reversal 1 1,50,000 4 09:30 AM to 10:00 AM May 29, 2026 (Friday) 2. items.title: Quantum Secure and Adaptive Financial Ecosystem (Q-SAFE) – Setting up of an Expert Committee
items.summary: Quantum Technology represents a paradigm shift compared with traditional systems as it leverages the principles of quantum mechanics, such as superposition and entanglement. This enables quantum systems to address complex financial problems such as portfolio optimisation, risk assessment, macroeconomic modelling, etc. However, quantum technologies can also introduce significant risks, particularly the potential to undermine certain existing cryptographic standards. 2. To examine the related issues, it has been decided to constitute an Expert Committee for a Quantum Secure and Adaptive Financia

items.title: RBI issues final Amendment Directions on Cooling-off Period for Directors of Co-operative Banks
items.summary: The Reserve Bank had, on January 8, 2026, issued Draft Reserve Bank of India (Urban Co-operative Banks - Governance) Amendment Directions, 2026 and Draft Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Governance) Amendment Directions, 2026 , seeking feedback from stakeholders. The draft Amendment Directions proposed to introduce a cooling-off period for directors of UCBs and RCBs after they complete a continuous tenure of ten years on the Board of a UCB / RCB, to ensure that the statutory provision contained in section 10A(2A)(i) read with section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 is i

items.title: Brent crude rises 2% as U.S. military strikes against Iran cloud Middle East peace prospects
items.summary: Donald Trump had said negotiations with Iran were "proceeding nicely," while warning that the U.S. could resume military action if discussions were to collapse.

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Live: Sensex trades flat, Nifty above 24,000; Crude jumps over 1% after US strikes Iran
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices LIVE: India’s equity ​benchmarks opened lower on Tuesday, ‌as investors turned cautious ​after U.S. strikes ⁠on southern Iran targeted boats allegedly attempting to lay mines ‌and missile-launch sites.

items.title: Q4 Results Today Live: ONGC, EID Parry, Brainbees, JK Tyre, GIC, IRCTC, Gujarat Fluorochemicals, AIA Eng, Gujarat Gas, Sumitomo Chemical to announce Q4 results, Shriram Properties, Suzlon, NBCC, Yatharth Hospital, Ajmera Realty shares in focus
items.summary: Q4 Results Today Live Updates: Stay tuned to businessline for the latest live updates on Q4 earnings.

items.title: Small-cap stock Kellton Tech Solutions rises 7% after receipt of an order from the UAE
items.summary: Kellton Tech Solutions' stock rose over 7% after securing an order from a UAE enterprise group for developing a unified digital operating platform. This project aims to enhance operational efficiency and strengthen Kellton's position in the Middle East technology market.

items.title: Kent RO delays IPO as Iran-US war dents sentiment
items.summary: Chairman and ‌Managing Director Mahesh Gupta ruled ​out an IPO for at least a year, but said the company could revisit ⁠plans once conditions stabilise.

items.title: ONGC Q4 Results 2026 LIVE: From profit, oil sales to dividend- what to expect from oil PSU amid rising crude oil prices?
items.summary: ONGC Q4 Results 2026 LIVE: ONGC is expected to report strong earnings growth in the fourth quarter of FY26 on a year-on-year (YoY), led by higher oil realizations as crude oil prices jumped during the quarter following disruptions around the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Pine Labs shares slide 1% as Q4 results miss estimates; analysts stay cautious
items.summary: Morgan Stanley flagged that the international business was hurt by Middle East geopolitical tensions, which disrupted airline volumes and delayed product rollouts with UAE banking partners

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: Markets optimism tested by fresh Iran strikes
items.summary: U.S. officials suggested a deal with Iran is near, but strikes continue, creating a complex trading picture for Wall Street's reopening.

items.title: Ashish Kacholia portfolio stock Balu Forge jumps 8% after announcement to foray in aerospace business
items.summary: Balu Forge share price jumped nearly 8% on May 26 after securing its first aerospace order from Alpha Aircraft Systems Inc. This milestone marks Balu Forge's entry into the aerospace sector, with development and manufacturing now underway following an advance payment received on May 25, 2026.

items.title: India, US seal critical minerals and rare earths pact amid global supply chain race
items.summary: India and the US on Tuesday signed a critical minerals and rare earths agreement aimed at strengthening supply chains for key industrial resources used in clean energy, electronics and defence sectors. The pact comes amid intensifying global efforts to diversify sourcing and reduce dependence on China’s dominance in rare earth processing and supplies.

items.title: Sri Lanka jolts markets with outsized 100-bp rate hike to counter Gulf crisis
items.summary: Sri Lanka's central bank enacted a significant 100 basis point policy rate hike, its largest in four years, to combat rising inflation and a weakening rupee. This move comes as the nation grapples with soaring energy prices driven by the Middle East conflict, impacting economic growth and foreign reserves.

items.title: ACME Solar signs power purchase pact with SECI
items.summary: ACME Solar has secured a power purchase agreement with Solar Energy Corporation of India. This deal is for a 300 MW/1,200 MWh project spanning 25 years. The project will supply assured peak power during non-solar hours. This will boost ACME Solar's contracted portfolio to 6,570 MW.

items.title: Indian bonds choppy as oil reverses course putting focus back on US-Iran issues
items.summary: Indian government bond yields edged higher in early trading Tuesday, influenced by renewed U.S.-Iran tensions that pushed oil prices up. This development reversed the support from softer Treasury yields, creating a choppy market environment. Traders anticipate a narrow trading range for bonds, with Friday's auction potentially swaying sentiment.

items.title: Policy stability, relief on STT and LTCG key to winning back FPIs: Sudip Bandyopadhyay
items.summary: Foreign investors are pulling money from Indian markets. Experts urge India to rethink taxes like STT and LTCG. Policy consistency is also a major concern for global funds. While domestic investors provide support, foreign capital remains vital for growth. Adjusting these tax structures could boost investor confidence and market stability.

items.title: You can't control the speed of innovation, says LTM CEO Lambu
items.summary: LTM’s first year under chief executive Venu Lambu has been marked by big bets on growth, even as the sector grapples with uncertainty around AI. As LTM advances its goal of doubling revenue, he says enterprises will continue to spend if AI investments deliver on the context, cost and mindset change.

items.title: 'An £8,000 debt pushed me to breaking point'
items.summary: A Bradford man who has struggled with debt urges others to reach out for help.

items.title: Aditya Shah calls for complete removal of LTCG for foreign investors amid FPI outflows
items.summary: Aditya Shah of Hercules Advisors urges India to abolish its long-term capital gains tax on equities for foreign investors, arguing it deters crucial portfolio inflows. He contends that removing the tax is the only credible signal to attract foreign capital, which is vital for lowering the cost of capital and deepening markets.

items.title: RVNL shares fall 4% after Q4 profit drops 43% y-o-y
items.summary: The stock traded at ₹264.45 on the NSE at 10.14 am, after hitting an intraday low of ₹261.05 against the previous close of ₹272.45.

items.title: ​How Mastercard became one of Wall Street’s greatest wealth creators
items.summary: Mastercard’s two-decade rally showcases digital payment dominance, scalable networks, strong competitive advantages, and long-term compounding. Expanding beyond card payments into cybersecurity and AI, Mastercard continues shaping global commerce and investor wealth creation.

items.title: Bank holiday sun boosts South West tourism
items.summary: Business owners in Devon and Cornwall describe how "the sun just brings everybody out".

items.title: Crude oil prices rebound after 7% fall on fresh US strikes on Iran; Brent above $98/bbl. What lies ahead?
items.summary: Crude oil prices on MCX rose as much as 1.90% to ₹8,779 per barrel amid doubts over the prospects of an interim agreement between Tehran and Washington to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Indraprastha Gas share price jumps 6% on CNG price hike
items.summary: Indraprastha Gas shares rose nearly 6% on May 26 after a CNG price hike of ₹2/kg in Delhi. This marks the fourth increase in two weeks, with prices now at ₹83.09/kg. Other regions also saw adjustments amid rising global energy costs.

items.title: India’s silver market faces purity crisis amid soaring investment demand
items.summary: Counterfeiters are flooding the Indian market with impure silver, exploiting rising prices and demand. Refiners are urging mandatory hallmarking and licensing for silver products to ensure quality and transparency. Despite upcoming mandatory hallmarking, many jewelers are not complying, leading to substandard silver with prohibited elements entering the market.

items.title: ITC dividend record date tomorrow: Last date to buy the FMCG stock for ₹8 dividend
items.summary: ITC dividend record date is set as May 27, Wednesday. According to the T+1 settlement, investors who buy ITC shares today will likely receive them in their demat accounts by tomorrow, May 27. Hence, they will be eligible for the ITC dividend.

items.title: Awfis Space Solutions shares surge 9% after Q4 profit jumps 107% YoY
items.summary: Awfis Space Solutions shares saw a significant jump after the company announced a more than two-fold increase in net profit for the March 2026 quarter. Strong demand for managed office spaces and expansion across India drove this performance. The company reported robust revenue growth and improved operational efficiency.

items.title: Be stock-specific, look for bargains: Daljeet Kohli on Nifty at 24,000 and top sector picks
items.summary: Market expert Daljeet Kohli advises investors to focus on quality stocks at discounted prices, rather than the Nifty's movement. He highlights opportunities in pharmaceuticals, particularly the semaglutide market, and the power sector. Kohli also suggests selective investments in autos, mid-cap banks, and housing finance, while cautioning against oil marketing companies.

items.title: Teamtech Formwork Solutions shares off to a solid start, list at 19% premium over IPO price on NSE SME
items.summary: Teamtech Formwork Solutions shares opened at ₹75 today compared with its initial public offering (IPO) price of ₹63, recording a listing pop of ₹12 or 19.05%.

items.title: Stocks to buy for short term: NMDC, Bank of India among 5 stocks experts recommend for next 1-2 weeks
items.summary: Stocks to buy for the short term: Market analysts recommend five promising stocks for the next 1-2 weeks, highlighting bullish trends and strong support zones. Stocks like Bank of India and Thyrocare Technologies show potential for growth, backed by robust technical indicators.

items.title: India's prized Alphonso mango crop ruined by weather
items.summary: A sharp difference in day and nighttime temperatures in December and January hurt flowering and fruit setting, while hotter than usual weather in April and May, probably due to the El Nino weather phenomenon, ​then spoiled the fruits themselves, an official said.

items.title: Crude oil futures rise after reports of US attacks in Southern Iran
items.summary: Trump in a social media post said talks with Iran were proceeding, and that there would ‘only be a Great Deal for all, or no deal at all!’

items.title: US Stock Market: Bond yields drop after Trump signals progress in Iran talks
items.summary: Treasury yields declined as optimism around a US-Iran agreement boosted demand for government bonds. Softer oil prices eased inflation concerns, reducing pressure on the Federal Reserve to maintain aggressive monetary policy. However, market caution persisted following reports of US and Israeli strikes on Iranian vessels.

items.title: ‘More SIP isn’t bad for us’: Deepak Shenoy says stopping mutual fund SIPs could hurt India more than FII outflows
items.summary: India's SIP culture has sparked debate over foreign investor exits and the weakening rupee. Market expert Deepak Shenoy defends SIPs, arguing that domestic participation fosters economic growth and liquidity, countering claims that retail buying pressures the rupee.

items.title: China, Pakistan reach 'new broad consensus' on boosting ties
items.summary: China and Pakistan have forged a new understanding to strengthen their strategic alliance. Both nations will focus on advancing the China-Pakistan Economic Corridor's development. This initiative will now be open to participation from other countries. This agreement marks a significant step in bilateral cooperation.

items.title: Rupee falls 17 paise to 95.43 against US dollar in early trade
items.summary: Forex traders said the broader pressure on the rupee is likely to continue as long as geopolitical tensions remain elevated.

items.title: Stocks to buy: Sagar Doshi suggests Bandhan Bank, Metropolis Healthcare, CESC shares to buy
items.summary: Indian benchmark indices opened lower on May 26 as investors reacted cautiously to US military actions in Iran. The Nifty 50 fell 0.11% and the Sensex declined 0.35%, amid rising oil prices and ongoing geopolitical uncertainty affecting market sentiment.

items.title: Global Market: Chinese AI and chip stocks rally after Huawei signals major tech leap
items.summary: Chinese semiconductor stocks surged in Hong Kong following Huawei's announcement of a potential 1.4nm chip breakthrough using "LogicFolding" technology by 2031. This development aims to narrow the gap with global leaders and bolster China's AI ambitions amid US restrictions. While promising, the technology's commercial viability remains to be seen, with a key test expected with upcoming Kirin mobile chips.

items.title: Jefferies raises target price of auto ancillary stock that's venturing into aerospace
items.summary: Jefferies has increased its target price for Belrise Industries to Rs 250, maintaining a positive outlook. The auto component maker is experiencing strong earnings growth and securing new orders. Belrise is also expanding its global presence in aerospace and defence sectors. This strategic diversification is expected to drive future growth and profitability for the company.

items.title: Pine Labs shares rise over 4% after Q4 results. Why Morgan Stanley forecasts 67% upside
items.summary: Pine Labs shares surged after the company reported a significant turnaround, posting a Rs 59 crore net profit in Q4FY26 against a loss last year. Full-year profits also swung to Rs 113 crore. Despite a recent share price dip, Morgan Stanley maintains an "Equal-weight" rating with a Rs 245 target, citing strong business growth.

items.title: Consumers unlikely to see major impact on food prices over next 2-3 months: Consumer Affairs Secy
items.summary: The government is closely monitoring the prices of essential food commodities. At present, prices have remained stable and largely range-bound, says Consumer Affairs Secretary Nidhi Khare.

items.title: US Stock Market: SpaceX, OpenAI IPO excitement revives debate over lofty valuations
items.summary: A Reuters analysis of the 50 largest IPOs over the past five years found that most underperformed the S&P 500 despite strong debut enthusiasm. As investors await SpaceX’s expected blockbuster listing, analysts caution that high-profile IPOs often struggle to deliver sustained market-beating returns after going public.

items.title: US Stock Market: Kevin Warsh’s Fed debut signals major shift in US monetary policy landscape
items.summary: President Donald Trump now directly influences US economic policy with Kevin Warsh leading the Federal Reserve. This change comes as inflation rises and consumer confidence falls. Housing affordability is a major concern with mortgage rates climbing. Warsh faces pressure to balance growth with inflation control. The Federal Reserve's decisions will now be closely watched by Wall Street and voters.

items.title: Canada to send trade mission to India later this year, says minister
items.summary: Canada is sending a business delegation to India this year. This visit aims to significantly boost economic ties between the two nations. Both countries are working towards tripling their trade to reach USD 50 billion by 2030.

items.title: Markets open flat after Monday surge; crude jumps on fresh US strikes in Iran
items.summary: Sensex surged 1,073 points and the Nifty climbed 312 points to close above 24,000 in the previous session

items.title: Suzlon Energy shares rise after Q4 results. What are Motilal Oswal, Nuvama saying?
items.summary: Suzlon Energy shares rose on the BSE on Monday despite the company reporting a 6% year-on-year decline in Q4 consolidated net profit to Rs 1,114 crore. Revenue from operations surged 45% YoY to Rs 5,468 crore, while profit jumped 150% sequentially from the December quarter.

items.title: Q&A: Anna Gomez is the sole Democrat on the FCC. She has a warning for big media companies
items.summary: Q&A: Anna Gomez is the sole Democrat on the FCC. She has a warning for big media companies

items.title: Sensex slips 250 pts, Nifty at 24,000 as US strike on Iran dent sentiment; mid, smallcaps outperform
items.summary: Benchmark indices Nifty and Sensex traded marginally lower on Tuesday, impacted by US attacks in southern Iran, reigniting fears of a prolonged conflict. Despite ongoing negotiations, markets remain cautiously optimistic, with Brent crude prices stabilizing and equities showing resilience, as evidenced by Monday's Sensex rally.

items.title: MTAR Tech vs Apollo Micro Systems: Which multibagger defence stock to buy after 395% rally in 1 year
items.summary: Two Indian defence stocks, MTAR Tech and Apollo Micro Systems, have delivered spectacular returns. MTAR Tech surged 395% and Apollo Micro Systems climbed 185% in a year. Analysts are now evaluating which stock offers better future prospects. Both companies operate in a booming defence sector with strong order books and growth potential.

items.title: Monzo spends heavily on ‘refer a friend’ rewards to boost market share
items.summary: London-based fintech records ‘staggering’ increase in spending on referral fees

items.title: Deregulation hands top US and UK banks $1.3tn opening
items.summary: Regulatory loosening has allowed countries’ banks to expand balance sheets while EU and Swiss rivals are constrained

items.title: The UK’s best-performing bank is probably not the one you think
items.summary: Shares in London-listed but Georgia-based Lion Finance have risen 800% since 2021

items.title: Stocks pare gain, oil climbs as US strikes Iran: Markets Wrap
items.summary: The dollar strengthened against all its Group-of-10 peers, while gold erased earlier gains to fall 0.5% to about $4,545 an ounce

items.title: South Korea's Kospi hits new high amid mixed trading in Asia
items.summary: Asia-Pacific markets were set to open broadly higher Tuesday, amid hopes for a breakthrough in the U.S.-Iran peace negotiations.

items.title: Silver dips Rs 3,800/kg, gold at Rs 1.58 lakh/10 gms after US attacks southern Iran in fresh strikes. Should you sell?
items.summary: Gold and silver prices on the MCX opened lower on Tuesday as renewed U.S. strikes in Iran pushed oil prices higher, fueling concerns over sticky inflation and prolonged elevated interest rates. The weakness followed U.S. military strikes on targets in southern Iran, including missile launch sites and boats allegedly involved in laying mines, aimed at safeguarding American troops from Iranian threats.

items.title: RVNL share price tanks 4% after weak Q4 results 2026. Should you buy or sell?
items.summary: RVNL share price falls 4% on Tuesday, 26 May, after the company reported its Q4 results on Monday. Here are the results highlights, along with experts' opinion on whether you should buy or sell the railway stock.

items.title: As doomsday predictions become routine, India's forgotten smallcaps dole out 7 multibaggers in just 60 days
items.summary: India's smallcap stocks have staged a remarkable comeback, delivering multibagger returns in just two months. Many beaten-down companies have surged significantly after the March market correction. This rally is driven by improving risk appetite, stabilizing crude oil prices, and strong quarterly earnings. Domestic investors are returning to selective buying, reigniting interest in this segment.

items.title: Adani Green commissions 3.37 GWh battery storage system at Khavda, scales up key renewable energy hub
items.summary: Adani Green Energy has commissioned a large battery storage system in Khavda, Gujarat. This facility is part of India's clean energy push. The system can power many homes or meet peak demand for cities. Adani Green Energy plans further expansion of its storage capacity in the coming years. The Khavda site is key to their renewable energy growth.

items.title: Gold price drops on MCX due to profit booking as US-Iran conflict stokes inflation fears
items.summary: Gold price on MCX dropped amid profit booking as the US-Iran conflict stoked inflation fears, raising expectations of interest rate hikes in the US and India.

items.title: Silver rate today drops over 1% to ₹2.72 lakh/kg on MCX on firm dollar, high crude prices amid US-Iran tensions
items.summary: On MCX, silver rate fell 1.3% to ₹2,72,985 per kg, while MCX Gold price lost 0.34% to ₹1,58,534 per 10 grams.

items.title: Rupee falls 15 paise to open at 95.38 against US dollar
items.summary: The Indian rupee fell 15 paise to 95.38 against the US dollar due to geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and expected month-end dollar demand. Brent crude rose over 2% amid concerns of escalation after US military actions in Iran.

items.title: Premier Energies shares gain 4% after Nomura, Quant, others purchase stake in Rs 2,289 crore bulk deal
items.summary: Premier Energies shares climbed 4% following a large bulk deal. The promoter family divested shares worth approximately Rs 2,289 crore. Several institutional investors, including Smallcap World Fund and Quant Mutual Fund, acquired substantial stakes. This activity highlights strong investor interest in the renewable energy sector and domestic solar manufacturing.

items.title: After a milestone year, realty's big four aim for ₹1.2 tn home sales in FY27
items.summary: Though the FY27 sales targets of the four developers show a modest increase of around 13% from last year’s high sales achievement, analysts believe there would be several factors at play.

items.title: Sebi may ease margin norms for certain MTF trades
items.summary: The SMAC’s proposal seeks to reduce this higher maintenance requirement to VaR plus three times ELM even in cases where cash collateral is used for pay-in, effectively lowering the margin burden on such trades.

items.title: The next phase of India manufacturing: HDFC AMC’s Rakesh Sethia breaks down real winners in EMS, aerospace & auto
items.summary: India's manufacturing sector is transitioning to deep tech localization, fueled by robust domestic demand and government incentives. HDFC AMC's Rakesh Sethia highlights structural winners in EMS, aerospace, and auto ancillaries, emphasizing the need for careful stock selection amidst rising valuations. The focus is on companies with strong execution, quality businesses, and export relevance.

items.title: Cautious on valuations, bullish on insurance and IT: How Nilesh Shetty is positioning for the next 12 months
items.summary: A cautious Nilesh Shetty of Quantum Advisors warns of a possible earnings disappointment in the next three to six months, citing El Niño risks, IT job losses, and unabsorbed cost pressures. Despite the caution, he sees deep value in private banks, insurance, and large-cap IT. He is trimming metals, autos, and power stocks, which he considers overvalued.

items.title: Why Indian investors are looking at space economy ETFs ahead of SpaceX IPO: Subho Moulik of Appreciate decodes
items.summary: Indian investors are eyeing the global space economy ahead of the SpaceX IPO. With no pure-play listed space companies in India, global ETFs offer a route to invest. Investors can also participate directly in SpaceX through the Liberalised Remittance Scheme. Experts advise separating listing excitement from investment discipline. Long-term holding is recommended for tax benefits.

items.title: HDFC Bank, SBI among 10 stocks that saw highest selling by LIC in Q4. Do you own any?​
items.summary: Life Insurance Corporation of India has significantly reduced its holdings in several major listed companies during the latest quarter. Notable among these are HDFC Bank and State Bank of India, with LIC divesting substantial amounts. These portfolio adjustments offer insights into the insurer's strategic repositioning amidst market volatility.​

items.title: Pine Labs among 6 stocks closed crossing above VWAP
items.summary: Six stocks from the Nifty500 universe closed over 2% above their VWAP on May 25, indicating strong intraday buying activity and improving market sentiment. A close above VWAP reflects price strength during the session and is considered a short-term bullish signal, suggesting sustained accumulation and potential continuation of upward momentum.

items.title: Quad agenda set in motion: India, Japan align on Indo-Pacific strategy ahead of key meet
items.summary: Japanese Foreign Minister Toshimitsu Motegi met with Indian External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar. Discussions centered on strengthening bilateral ties and cooperation on global issues. Both nations agreed to boost economic security and promote investment. They also committed to advancing defense and security initiatives. The leaders confirmed cooperation for energy and critical material supply in Southeast and South Asia.

items.title: RVNL shares fall 3% as Q4 net profit tumbles 43%; revenue rises just 5%
items.summary: Rail Vikas Nigam Limited (RVNL) shares dipped 3% after reporting a 43% year-on-year decline in standalone net profit to Rs 212 crore for the March quarter. Despite a 5% revenue increase to Rs 6,648 crore, expenses rose significantly, impacting profitability. Full-year profit also saw a 33% drop.

items.title: Adani Power among 5 stocks flashing bullish signals, hinting at a possible uptrend
items.summary: Five Nifty500 stocks appeared on the White Marubozu bullish scanner on May 25, signalling strong buying momentum. The candlestick pattern reflects sustained bullish pressure throughout the session, indicating buyers dominated price action and suggesting a potential continuation of the uptrend in the near term across select counters.

items.title: After stellar Q4, is a downgrade nightmare looming for India Inc in Q1?
items.summary: India Inc. showed strength in Q4 earnings. However, a Q1 FY27 earnings downgrade cycle is now a significant market concern. Elevated crude prices, a weaker rupee, and rising inflation are pressuring corporate profits. Analysts are watching closely to see if companies can absorb these shocks or if a prolonged downgrade cycle will impact foreign investment.

items.title: D-Day at Tata boardroom today: Who blinks first as loss-making units, leadership tensions take centre stage
items.summary: The Tata Sons board meets on Tuesday to review loss-making businesses, with group losses projected to reach ₹29,000 crore. Chandrasekaran's reappointment is not on the agenda, but the tensions at the top are impossible to ignore.

items.title: India raises CNG prices by Rs 2/kg across key regions for fourth time in two weeks
LIC raises stake in Central Bank of India to over 6% amid government OFS move
items.summary: Life Insurance Corporation of India increased its stake in Central Bank of India to 6.06% by acquiring 26.26 crore equity shares. The bank also announced an oversubscription option in its ongoing offer for sale, raising the total offer size to 72.41 crore shares.

items.title: Q4 results 2026: IRCTC to ONGC among companies to declare Q4 results today; check full list here
items.summary: Q4 results 2026: More than 270 companies are scheduled to declare their Q4 results 2026 today. Here's a full list of companies to release results, along with preview.

items.title: Taiwan overtakes India as world's fifth-largest stock market
items.summary: Taiwan overtook India in stock market value, powered mainly by a breakneck rally in the world’s largest chipmaker Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.

items.title: China Traders Run for Exit After Cross-Border Flow Crackdown
items.summary: Chinese investors are rushing to find alternative ways to buy and sell overseas equities after Beijing launched its most forceful crackdown on illicit cross-border stock trading to stem capital outflows.

items.title: Indian bonds may extend gains, but fresh US-Iran escalation could reverse moves
items.summary: The benchmark 6.48% 2035 bond yield is expected to move in the 7.00% ‌to 7.06% range, a private bank trader said. It had ended at 7.0270% on ‌Monday.

items.title: U.S. conducts 'self-defense strikes' in Iran as Trump pushes for peace deal
items.summary: Targets included missile launch sites and Iranian boats attempting to emplace mines.

items.title: Stocks to buy in 2026 for long term: NTPC, Hindalco among 5 stocks that could give 10-20% return
items.summary: We have collated a list of recommendations from top brokerage firms from ETNow and other sources.

items.title: Shares to buy or sell: Chandan Taparia of Motilal Oswal recommends three stocks to buy today - 26 May 2026
items.summary: Shares to buy or sell: Chandan Taparia has recommended three stocks to buy today, 26 May 2026 - PNB Housing Finance, Adani Power and Bharat Heavy Electricals Ltd (BHEL) shares.

items.title: LIC 1:1 bonus share explained: What 21 lakh shareholders need to know before buying on record date
items.summary: LIC has set May 29 as the record date for its maiden 1:1 bonus share issue, rewarding shareholders with one free share for each held. This move, capitalizing reserves, aims to boost liquidity. Investors must purchase shares by May 27 to be eligible for this significant corporate action.

items.title: Ferrari unveils first fully electric car
items.summary: The new Luce model has divided opinion on social media, and comes despite intense pressure from Chinese EV makers.

items.title: ITC dividend alert! Last date to buy FMCG major’s shares for Rs 8 dividend. Do you own?
items.summary: ITC has set May 27 as the record date for its final dividend of Rs 8 per equity share for FY26, making today the last chance for investors to buy shares for eligibility. This marks the largest dividend in nearly six years, with the total payout for FY26 reaching Rs 14.50 per share.

items.title: Piyush Goyal says India, Canada aim to seal CEPA by year-end; target $50 billion trade by 2030
items.summary: India and Canada are working to finalize a trade agreement this year. Both nations aim to significantly boost economic cooperation. Bilateral trade is targeted to reach fifty billion dollars by twenty thirty. This renewed engagement follows recent high-level visits. Discussions also cover energy, agriculture, and technology partnerships. A large Indian business delegation is participating in the ongoing talks.

items.title: Negative opening seen for Indian stocks
items.summary: F&O settlements, holiday to keep market under pressure

items.title: Stocks to watch: RVNL, ONGC, BEL among 10 shares in focus today; check list here
items.summary: Stock market today: The Indian stock market is likely to snap its gaining streak as trends in the Gift Nifty index signalled a negative opening on Tuesday. Amid this backdrop, these stocks will remain in focus on 26 May.

items.title: JSW Energy raises ₹4,000 cr via QIP
items.summary: The company's board has approved the allocation of 7,61,90,476 equity shares at an issue price of ₹525 apiece, which is at a discount of ₹9.05 per share

items.title: Buy or sell: Gift Nifty signals positive start, Vaishali Parekh recommends three stocks to buy today — 26 May 2026
items.summary: Buy or sell stocks, 26 May 2026: Vaishali Parekh recommends three stocks to buy today — Tata Power, SRF, and Ujjivan Small Finance Bank

items.title: HDFC Mutual Fund buys 10 lakh shares of Global Health for over ₹122 cr
items.summary: The shares were picked up at an average price of ₹1,225 apiece, taking the transaction value to ₹122.50 crore.

items.title: Raja Venkatraman, MarketSmith recommend five stocks for 26 May
items.summary: Indian stock markets ended sharply higher on May 26, with the Sensex up 1,073.61 points and Nifty rising 312.40 points. Key drivers included falling crude oil prices, favorable global market signals, and a stronger Indian rupee, despite risks from upcoming monthly F&O expiry.

items.title: Nifty 50, Sensex prediction today: Check how Indian stock market is expected to trade on 26 May
items.summary: Nifty 50, Sensex prediction today: The trends on Gift Nifty also indicate a muted start for the Indian benchmark index. The Gift Nifty was trading around 24,057 level, a discount of nearly 6 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close.

items.title: Asian markets today: South Korea's Kospi hit record high amid US-Iran peace talks
items.summary: Asian markets today: South Korea's Kospi advanced to an intraday high of 8,094.90, while the small-cap Kosdaq gained 2.12% in early trading. Here's what's driving Asian markets today, along with update in US-Iran peace talks.

items.title: Treasuries Gain in Catch-Up Trade as Trump Sees Progress on Iran
items.summary: Treasuries rallied across the curve as cash trading resumed after a holiday, with investors growing more optimistic about a potential US-Iran deal.

items.title: Buzzing stocks: BEL, Paytm, JSW Energy, NLC India, Fredun Pharma, Raymond, Pasupathi Acrylon, Innovision, Bhageria Ind
From Gift Nifty, US-Iran talks, oil prices to Kospi rally: 8 key things that changed for Indian stock market overnight
items.summary: Gift Nifty was trading around 24,068 level, a premium of nearly 5 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close, indicating a flat start for the Indian stock market indices.

items.title: Gold slips as US-Iran tensions lift oil, stoke inflation fears
items.summary: GLOBAL-PRECIOUS:Gold slips as US-Iran tensions lift oil, stoke inflation fears

items.title: Breakout stocks to buy or sell: Sumeet Bagadia recommends five shares to buy today — 26 May 2026
items.summary: The Indian stock market ended Monday's session on a higher note. Sumeet Bagadia of Choice Broking recommends five breakout stocks to buy today.

items.title: Social media as bad for young people as smoking, top doctors say
items.summary: The Academy of Medical Royal Colleges says doctors should routinely check on screen time and social media use when seeing younger patients.

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: Faith in markets, doubts in diplomacy
items.summary: Markets remain optimistic as talks inch toward an Iran deal.

items.title: TSMC’s Relentless Rise Powers Taiwan’s Market Value Above India
items.summary: Taiwan overtook India in stock market value, powered mainly by a breakneck rally in the world’s largest chipmaker Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.

items.title: BMIP is India’s sovereign shield against maritime risks: GIC Re's Joshi
items.summary: Global reinsurers have a track record of issuing cancellation notices for war-risk covers during conflict zones, leading to sudden unavailability of cover or sharp spikes in premiums. This creates serious risks for India, one of the world’s largest crude oil importers and a major trading economy.

items.title: Wilmar International chief says India must target 60% self-sufficiency in edible oils
items.summary: India needs to produce more edible oils domestically. Kuok Khoon Hong, chairman of Wilmar International, suggests aiming for 50-60% self-sufficiency. This move is crucial given India's vast consumption and current reliance on imports. Stable government policies are key to encouraging local oilseed cultivation. Global factors are also driving up cooking oil prices.

items.title: Truck operators reel as diesel crunch pushes freight rates higher
items.summary: Nearly 20% of India's trucks are off the roads. Diesel shortages and price hikes are crippling the transport sector. Small operators are hit hardest. Freight rates are increasing, threatening to raise prices for food and other goods. Oil companies state shortages are localized. Demand is expected to ease with the monsoon.

items.title: Next boss warns of 'dramatic' fall in entry-level jobs
items.summary: Lord Wolfson tells the BBC Next now typically receives double the number of applicants for one role than it did two years ago.

items.title: The rise of the fruit that tastes like custard
items.summary: Custard apple plants are prized for their hardiness but exporting their delicate fruit is difficult.

items.title: CNBC's The China Connection newsletter: Tariffs eased. Trust didn't.
items.summary: As the largest consumer market globally, the U.S. still remains a prized destination for Chinese companies that are finding ways to address local concerns.

items.title: The Father of Japan’s Conbini Was Right to Ignore His Critics
items.summary: Japan’s conbini are beloved — a lifeline for the country’s overworked parents and hungry salarymen as well as a must-see experience for tourists.

items.title: SBI-led banks seek bids for Essar Group promoter guarantees
items.summary: The reserve price for the bids, with a June 17 deadline, is Rs 200 crore, showed a document on the website of the process advisor, BoB Capital Markets. It said the residual liabilities represent the net balance remaining following the mandatory deduction of recoveries realised under the resolution plan whereby ArcelorMittal India had taken over Essar Steel in a bankruptcy administration.

items.title: Solar surge hits coal wall, 300 GWh of clean power wasted in Q1 of 2026
items.summary: Coal-fired power plants in India are reportedly blocking the evacuation of solar power into the grid, leading to significant renewable energy curtailment.

items.title: ONGC picks BP as tech partner for Western Offshore fields
items.summary: BP already has a similar arrangement for ONGC's flagship Mumbai High fields, but the latest move significantly expands its role, making it responsible for helping enhance output from fields that account for about 72% of ONGC's gas production and 60% of its oil output.

items.title: India's fertilizer subsidy may cross ₹3 lakh crore if West Asia crisis prolongs
items.summary: India’s fertiliser subsidy bill could surge past a record ₹3 lakh crore this fiscal if the West Asia crisis continues, sharply exceeding the budget estimate of ₹1.71 lakh crore, a government official said.

items.title: OMCs hike fuel prices, this time by ₹2.7 a litre
items.summary: State-run oil marketing companies have increased petrol and diesel prices by about ₹2.7 per litre, marking the fourth hike in 11 days and pushing the total rise to around ₹7.5–8 per litre.

items.title: IRFC refinances Hyderabad metro's ₹13,527 crore debt
items.summary: Indian Railway Finance Corporation has entered metro rail financing. It signed a term loan agreement to refinance ₹13,527 crore debt for the Hyderabad Metro Rail project. This move is expected to help the project report a profit next year. Hyderabad Metro's earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization are already positive.

items.title: CD rates on the rise could soon bring more on retail deposits
items.summary: Bank deposit rates are set to rise. Costs for certificates of deposit have increased, signaling banks will soon offer better returns to savers. This is driven by tighter liquidity and higher demand for funds. Experts anticipate deposit rates will move upwards, even without an immediate policy rate hike from the Reserve Bank of India.

items.title: Competition heats up in India’s smartphone manufacturing sector as PLI winds down
items.summary: India's Android smartphone manufacturing is heating up. As a key government scheme concludes, companies are competing fiercely on price. Brands are now spreading their orders across more manufacturers. This shift is impacting major players, with mid-tier rivals gaining ground. Companies are actively seeking new business to drive growth in a market facing stagnant volumes.

items.title: India to revamp IIP with new base year, wider coverage from June 1
items.summary: India will launch an updated Index of Industrial Production on June 1. The new IIP will feature a 2022-23 base year and include sectors like minor minerals and waste management. It will offer more detailed tracking of industrial activity. This revamp aims to provide a clearer picture of India's industrial growth.

items.title: India, Japan discuss steps to fix energy supply disruptions
items.summary: India and Japan are addressing disruptions in energy supply chains and maritime connectivity. External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar met his Japanese counterpart Toshimitsu Motegi. They discussed bilateral cooperation and global issues. Both nations are energy importing and big trading countries. Economic security is a key concern for both. They aim to advance industrial and technological collaboration in critical sectors.

items.title: Centre plans digital trail for fertiliser subsidies via AgriStack
items.summary: The government is set to link fertiliser sales with the national AgriStack database. This digital initiative aims to prevent the misuse of subsidised nutrients and monitor farm-level usage. Authorities will match fertiliser purchases with farmer IDs and land details. This will help identify excessive buying and estimate nutrient needs.

items.title: Discoms told to commission rooftop solar units by May 31
items.summary: Electricity distribution companies must finish rooftop solar project inspections by May 31. This ensures projects already installed remain exempt from a new domestic solar cell rule starting June 1. Delays in certification could make projects non-compliant. The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy issued this advisory to avoid issues for project owners. This move supports domestic solar module manufacturing.

items.title: Government assures 78 days of oil amid MPs' concerns
items.summary: Concerns over fuel, shipping, and fertilizer supplies dominated a Parliamentary panel meeting. Officials assured adequate fuel reserves and fertilizer availability for the Kharif season. However, 37 Indian ships are stranded in West Asia due to the Strait of Hormuz closure. Cargo insurance premiums have risen sharply. The government is addressing these issues amid ongoing global challenges.

items.title: Big infra projects clock Rs 5.6 lakh crore cost overrun
items.summary: Major infrastructure projects in India have seen significant cost overruns. Projects valued at ₹150 crore or more have collectively exceeded their original budgets by ₹5.6 lakh crore. The total revised cost for 1,981 projects now stands at ₹42.8 lakh crore. Despite these overruns, expenditure on these projects indicates steady implementation progress.

items.title: Shipping ministry working on reform-driven roadmap to accelerate maritime transformation
items.summary: India's shipping ministry is driving a major maritime transformation. A new roadmap focuses on better governance and ease of doing business. Officials are tasked with faster resolution of pending issues and grievances. A digital platform and mobile app will streamline services. The ministry is also embracing AI and data for improved efficiency and transparency across the sector.

items.title: Iran would open Strait of Hormuz 30 days after peace deal
items.summary: The U.S. and Iran are reportedly discussing a phased plan to reopen the Strait of Hormuz after a potential agreement to end hostilities, according to a Nikkei report citing a Middle East diplomatic source.

items.title: Govt open to views on capital gains tax cuts amid FPI outflows: Sitharaman
items.summary: FPIs were net sellers of securities worth a record ₹1.8 trillion in FY26, the highest in 34 years and up from ₹1.3 trillion in FY25. The highest outflows were seen in March 2026 at around ₹1.18 trillion.

items.title: BSNL revenue rises 19% to Rs 25,000 crore in two years; tariff cheaper than pvt telcos: MoS Telecom
items.summary: State-owned BSNL has seen a significant revenue jump of 19 percent, reaching Rs 25,000 crore in two years. This financial turnaround is attributed to improved infrastructure and operational efficiency. The company's EBITDA has also seen a dramatic increase.

items.title: The energy shock is not over yet
items.summary: Even with a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, supplies will take time to normalise

items.title: AU Real Estate acquires 5.6 acre land in Siddharth Vihar, for Rs 336 crore
items.summary: AU Real Estate has acquired a 5.6-acre land parcel in Siddharth Vihar, Ghaziabad through an Uttar Pradesh Awas Vikas Parishad auction for ₹336 crore. The project will be developed via its subsidiary Cosmos REIT Pvt. Ltd.

items.title: Gold offered at ₹450/g discount to Indian consumers after import duty hike
items.summary: The bullion trade is passing on the benefits of importing the yellow metal at lower duty to spur demand

items.title: RBI unlikely to hike rates soon despite inflation risk, rate action only in Dec if needed: Icra's Aditi Nayar
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is expected to hold off on interest rate hikes. Inflation risks are rising due to fuel prices and monsoon uncertainty. A rate increase is only likely by year-end if price pressures continue. The central bank will monitor for broader inflationary effects before acting. This cautious approach aims to balance economic growth and price stability.

items.title: Fuel price hikes narrow oil companies’ losses to ₹600 crore a day
items.summary: With Monday’s hike of ₹2.61 per litre, petrol prices crossed the ₹100-per-litre mark in the national capital to retail at ₹102.12 per litre in Delhi, according to data from Indian Oil Corporation.

items.title: Ebola scare: DGCA asks airlines make in-flight announcements, take other measures
items.summary: Aviation watchdog DGCA has issued new guidelines for airlines amid the Ebola outbreak in Africa. Carriers with flights to or from Uganda and Congo must collect self-declaration forms from passengers. In-flight announcements will inform travelers about Ebola symptoms and reporting procedures. Protocols for suspected cases onboard aircraft are also detailed. Airlines must maintain stocks of protective gear.

items.title: ‘Don 3’ row escalates as FWICE issues non-cooperation directive against Ranveer Singh; actor responds
items.summary: The Federation of Western India Cine Employees has issued a non-cooperation directive against actor Ranveer Singh. This action follows his alleged last-minute withdrawal from the film Don 3. The federation stated that significant pre-production funds were spent. Singh's representatives indicated a preference for legal channels over federation intervention. The federation remains open to resolution.

items.title: OMC stocks close sharply higher as Brent settles near $95 and fourth fuel hike shores up margins
items.summary: HPCL led gains with a 5.8% rise to ₹412.55 on the NSE, BPCL advanced over 4% and IOC was up 3.7%

items.title: Nifty crosses 24,000 on US-Iran peace hopes, crude plunge
items.summary: The Nifty 50 closed at 24,031.70, up 312.40 points (1.32%), marking its first close above 24,000 in recent sessions; The BSE Sensex ended at 76,488.96, gaining 1,073.61 points (1.42%)

items.title: Gold ETF investors press the exit button again after a week
items.summary: US, Chinese investors lead the outflows; Asian investments turn negative for the second week in a row

items.title: Q4 Results Highlights: Aditya Birla Fashion Q4 net loss widens, Ajmera Realty sales jump, Sundaram Finance, Hitachi Energy India profit up, Crizac hits upper circuit
items.summary: Q4 Results Highlights: Find all the latest update on the financial results of the companies scheduled to announce, here.

items.title: Hindustan Zinc’s Rampura Agucha mine gets Zinc Mark certification
items.summary: It is a globally recognised assurance framework that validates responsible zinc production against internationally benchmarked ESG standards

items.title: Toyota Kirloskar to invest Rs 1,200 crore at KWIN City in Karnataka
items.summary: Toyota Kirloskar Motor will establish its first global 'BizIntel Hub' in Karnataka's KWIN City, investing Rs 1,200 crore over five years. The 300-acre facility will focus on vehicle manufacturing, testing, and advanced automobile production, creating approximately 200 jobs. This marks the first major investment for the KWIN City project.

items.title: India's mine block operationalisation picks up
items.summary: India’s mining sector has seen a sharp acceleration in both auctions and operationalisation of mineral blocks in recent years, according to the mines ministry. In FY26 so far, 36 mineral blocks—28 greenfield and 8 brownfield—have been made operational.

items.title: RBI tightens norms for re-appointment of directors on UCB boards
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has introduced new rules for Urban Co-operative Banks. Directors can now serve for a maximum of ten years. Following this, a mandatory three-year cooling-off period is required before re-appointment. This measure aims to prevent directors from circumventing tenure limits. The new directions are effective immediately, ensuring better governance in these financial institutions.

items.title: Parliamentary panel members grill Oil ministry officials over 'shortage' of petrol, LPG
items.summary: Parliamentary panel members questioned Oil Ministry officials about petrol and diesel shortages. Officials confirmed crude oil stocks for 78 days. Thirteen Indian ships remain stranded in West Asia due to the Strait of Hormuz closure. The government is working to ensure no shortages, but war uncertainty persists. Fertiliser availability is also assured.

items.title: India emerges as Sri Lanka's second-largest export destination
items.summary: India is now Sri Lanka's second-largest export market, overtaking the United Kingdom. This shift occurred between January and April of this year. The United States remains the top destination for Sri Lankan exports. Sri Lanka's overall exports saw growth in April, driven by merchandise and services. Coconut products and IT services performed well.

items.title: Indian entrepreneur explains how Warren Buffett shaped his financial habits: ‘I don't have single rupee of black money’
items.summary: The entrepreneur credited Buffett as one of the biggest influences in his life and stressed the importance of choosing the right role models.

items.title: IRFC signs Rs 13,527 crore loan agreement with Hyderabad Metro for debt refinancing
items.summary: IRFC will provide a Rs 13,527 crore loan to Hyderabad Metro Rail. This refinancing will help clear debt obligations. IRFC expects to fund more metro projects. The company plans to raise Rs 70,000 crore this financial year. This move signals significant growth for metro infrastructure financing in India.

items.title: SC rules exxsol hexane a pure hydrocarbon, duty cut to 2.5%
items.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that exxsol hexane is a pure hydrocarbon. This decision means imported exxsol hexane will attract a lower customs duty of 2.5 percent. Previously, customs officials wanted it classified as motor spirit, which carried a 10 percent duty. The court upheld a tribunal's order favoring the lower duty. This ruling impacts the nation's import-export status.

items.title: Government forwent Rs 1 lakh crore via excise duty cuts to shield consumers from fuel inflation: FM
items.summary: India's Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman explains recent fuel price increases are market-driven by oil companies. She noted the government previously absorbed over Rs 1 lakh crore in revenue through excise duty cuts to protect consumers from inflation. The minister also discussed managing global challenges and the evolving landscape for textile exporters, emphasizing sustainability and technology adoption.

items.title: FM Sitharaman says Government open to hear investor concerns on LTCG, STCG taxation
items.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman has indicated the government is ready to listen to stock market investors. Concerns regarding Long-Term Capital Gains and Short-Term Capital Gains taxes are being considered. The Minister emphasized openness to feedback on taxation matters. This comes amid market volatility and discussions on investor sentiment. The government is engaging with stakeholders on market-related tax issues.

items.title: SEBI approves appointment of two executive directors at CDSL
items.summary: The appointments will take effect from their respective joining dates and remain subject to shareholder ratification, according to SEBI’s approval letters issued on Monday.

items.title: Government rules out blanket ALMM List-II extension beyond June 1; case-by-case relief for solar projects
items.summary: The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy will not extend the ALMM List-II deadline past June 1, 2026. Projects commissioned after this date must comply. Relief is available for projects with existing investments and completed installations. Developers can submit claims for extensions by June 30, 2026.

items.title: ONGC on-boards BP subsidiary as technical services provider to enhance production from western offshore
items.summary: The TSP will review the field performance and identify improvements in reservoir, facilities and wells to enhance production from western Offshore fields

items.title: India may end refined copper import dependence this year, says Hindalco MD
items.summary: The push for self-reliance in copper aligns with India’s broader industrial strategy, particularly as demand accelerates from energy transition sectors.

items.title: Broker’s Call: PNC Infratech (Accumulate)
items.summary: Elara Securities

items.title: Two LNG tankers pass through Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: Another vessel carrying crude oil crossed the vital shipping lane on Saturday

items.title: IRFC expands beyond Indian Railways with ₹13,527-crore refinancing deal for Hyderabad Metro
items.summary: The state-backed financier is leveraging its core strength in transportation finance to support urban mobility and help the Telangana government-owned project replace its high-cost debt.

items.title: SEBI proposes dynamic option strike price framework
items.summary: Exchanges may be required to introduce new option strikes intraday to ensure trading continuity during sharp market moves

items.title: Broker’s Call: Prestige Estates (Buy)
items.summary: Axis Securities

items.title: India proposes broad revamp of industrial output index ahead of new series
items.summary: India's statistics ministry is set to unveil a new way to measure industrial output next week. Key changes include expanding the sectors tracked and adopting a chain-linked framework. The base year will be updated, and new sectors like renewable power and waste management will be included. This aims to provide a more accurate picture of India's evolving industrial landscape.

items.title: Oil prices slide on hopes of US-Iran peace deal
items.summary: Trump said on Saturday that an agreement would include the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, without giving further details.

items.title: European stocks highest since March 2 as U.S.-Iran talks continue; euro zone bond yields drop on peace hopes
items.summary: Stocks in Europe are tracking their Asian counterparts higher after Japan's Nikkei 225 breached 65,000 for the first time.

items.title: FM asks lenders to go beyond standard loans, design credit repayments around biz cycles
items.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman has called on banks to move beyond standard loan products. She emphasized the need for repayment structures that align with the specific income cycles of different businesses. This initiative aims to provide tailored credit solutions for MSMEs, supporting their growth and contributing to a stronger middle class and a developed India.

items.title: Infra projects see cost overrun of Rs 5.65 lakh crore in April
items.summary: Central sector infrastructure projects exceeding Rs 150 crore each show a cumulative cost overrun of Rs 5.65 lakh crore. As of April, 1,981 projects are monitored with a revised cost of Rs 42.78 lakh crore. Expenditure stands at Rs 20.36 lakh crore, indicating steady implementation. Transport and logistics lead with the highest number of projects and revised costs.

items.title: RBI to examine role of quantum technology in strengthening financial sector
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is forming an expert committee to study quantum technology's role in finance. This new technology could revolutionize financial tasks but also poses security risks. The committee will assess current systems and recommend ways to make India's financial sector quantum-safe. The report is expected within six months.

items.title: India's wheat procurement surges past 34 million tonnes, bolsters food buffers
items.summary: Punjab led the procurement process, with around 12.16 mt of wheat purchased by government agencies, followed by Madhya Pradesh with 10.14 mt and Haryana 8.12 mt. Among the other wheat-producing states, Rajasthan and Uttar Pradesh purchased around 2.1 mt and 1.55 mt respectively until 24 May.

items.title: Rupee gains for third straight session, settles at 95.25 against dollar
items.summary: The rupee gained as Brent crude prices fell sharply and the dollar index weakened

items.title: VT Markets expands to 500+ US stocks with 39 new AI, Space and Energy picks
items.summary: The additions reflect a broader market shift from consumer-facing AI narratives toward the underlying infrastructure driving AI adoption — including optical networking, electricity generation, and data centre equipment

items.title: The 90% vulnerability: Inside Ajoy Chawla’s plan to grow Titan beyond jewellery
items.summary: A stellar quarter and ₹76,000 crore in annual revenue couldn’t save Titan from a brutal market crash. As gold tariffs rise and macro shocks hit Tanishq, can the retail giant successfully pivot to a diversified lifestyle future?

items.title: Zepto said to plan June public filing for $1 billion India IPO
items.summary: Deliberations are ongoing and details, including the size and timing of the IPO may still change

items.title: JM Financial launches second ₹1,000 crore credit AIF
items.summary: The fund aims to provide structured credit solutions to businesses while delivering risk-adjusted returns to investors

items.title: Adequate availability of fertilisers for upcoming Kharif sowing season: Govt
items.summary: India is set for a robust Kharif season with ample fertilizer supplies. Domestic production and imports have ensured comfortable stock levels. Over 200 lakh tonne of fertilizers are currently available, exceeding 50 percent of the projected demand. This proactive measure guarantees sufficient soil nutrient availability for farmers as sowing begins in June.

items.title: Beware the boom and bust cycle of memory stocks, investors warn amid AI excitement
items.summary: "In the long run, it's a pretty dreadful industry," William de Gale of BlueBox Asset Management, told CNBC's Europe Early Edition on Wednesday.

items.title: Italian bank CDP to raise stake in payments group Nexi after CVC weighs bid
items.summary: Cassa Depositi e Prestiti’s move comes as buyout firm had considered €9bn bid for Milan-listed group

items.title: PSU closure: Govt sets in motion new move after years wasted in stop-start process
items.summary: The Centre is pushing ministries to accelerate the closure of unprofitable state-owned companies. Progress has been slow despite a policy approved eight years ago. Only one company has effectively ceased operations. The government aims to expedite these closures, addressing concerns over delays in both disinvestment and shutdown processes.

items.title: West Asia conflict may dent India Inc profitability by 200 bps: Crisil
items.summary: A prolonged West Asia conflict poses a risk to India Inc's profitability. Crisil Ratings predicts a significant drop in operating margins due to supply chain issues and rising costs. However, strong company balance sheets are expected to protect overall credit quality. Some sectors like ceramics and airlines face severe stress. Other export-oriented sectors might benefit from a weaker rupee.

items.title: Schroders Capital plans to build on $1 bn India investment as scope for early investor exits grows beyond IPOs
items.summary: The company is scouting for opportunities in sectors including consumer, healthcare, technology and artificial intelligence.

items.title: Prize draw businesses have better luck than the average punter
items.summary: Lack of regulation has helped draw operators to thrive

items.title: India’s AI boom could turn data centres into the next big infrastructure race: KPMG
items.summary: India's data centre sector is set for massive growth, reaching nearly $46 billion by 2033. AI workloads and cloud adoption are key drivers. The market is shifting towards AI-optimised infrastructure. Challenges lie in execution complexity, with a call for integrated lifecycle partners. Data localisation laws and ESG priorities are also boosting demand for local data centres.

items.title: Singapore reports lower-than-expected inflation for April at 1.8%, revises economic growth higher
items.summary: Core inflation — which strips out prices of private transport and accommodation — came in at 1.4%, against estimates of 1.7%.

items.title: The three Fs that Sitharaman flagged as India braces for a widening global oil shock, forex strain
items.summary: In light of increasing global unrest from the West Asia crisis and fluctuations in the energy sector, Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman is advocating for a concentrated approach to managing fuel, fertiliser, and foreign exchange resources. Prime Minister Modi's recent appeals to conserve foreign currency and reduce discretionary imports underscore this strategy.

items.title: Japan’s Nikkei 225 tops 65,000 for first time as oil falls on Hormuz reopening hopes
items.summary: Japan’s Nikkei 225 breached 65,000 for the first time Monday as a sharp decline in oil prices lifted risk sentiment in holiday-thinned trading.

items.title: India turns net importer of finished steel in April, data shows
items.summary: India has become a net importer of finished steel. In April, imports rose significantly while exports also saw an increase. Major suppliers to India included China, South Korea, Japan, Vietnam and Russia. Domestic consumption of finished steel grew, and crude steel production also climbed.

items.title: FM Nirmala Sitharaman reveals petrol & diesel excise duty cut will hit govt revenues by ₹1 lakh crore
items.summary: Nirmala Sitharaman has confirmed that cutting excise duties on petrol and diesel will result in a revenue loss of ₹1 lakh crore. She also pushed back against a 'pessimistic narrative' on India's economy

items.title: AI wealth must benefit the public, South Korea's deputy PM says amid Samsung labor tensions
items.summary: South Korea's deputy PM tells CNBC there are concerns as to whether AI could worsen wealth gaps or lead to job losses.

items.title: Delivery Hero shares rise 10% as rival Uber mulls another takeover bid
items.summary: Delivery Hero confirmed in a statement on Saturday that it had received a takeover offer from Uber of €33 per share.

items.title: India to strengthen EV shield against oil imports with fresh subsidies: Report
items.summary: The government is set to significantly boost subsidies for electric two-wheelers. This move under the PM E-Drive scheme aims to promote clean mobility and reduce oil import dependence. The heavy industries ministry is seeking additional funds for this initiative. The scheme has already subsidized a large number of electric two-wheelers.

items.title: Iran is in the 'process of blinking' over the Strait of Hormuz, Petraeus says
items.summary: Ex-CIA director David Petraeus said that an initial successful peace deal with Tehran would see the Strait opened without any conditions.

items.title: Huawei plans new smartphone chips this fall as rivalry with Nvidia and Apple heats up
items.summary: Chinese telecom giant Huawei announced Monday a new way of designing chips that improves their capabilities, despite U.S. restrictions on the company.

items.title: Roseate Hotels targets asset-light luxury model with managed expansion push
items.summary: Roseate Hotels is shifting to an asset-light strategy and expanding into pilgrimage and lifestyle businesses as competition intensifies in India’s luxury hospitality sector.

items.title: Oil market at 'tank bottoms' in Asia, and Europe isn't far behind, warns market veteran Jeff Currie
items.summary: Oil markets are nearing minimum operating levels in Asia, with Europe likely next and the U.S. potentially facing shortages by July, said Carlyle's Jeff Currie.

items.title: 'I don't think I'll ever be able to retire'

items.title: India secures 2nd rank among EMs in April on oil-led export rebound
items.summary: Surging crude oil prices and disruptions around West Asia continued to test emerging economies in April, but India strengthened its position for the same reason.

items.title: Australian FM Penny Wong to visit India for Quad FMM
items.summary: Australian Foreign Minister Penny Wong will visit India for a crucial Quad Foreign Ministers' Meeting. She will also hold discussions with her Indian counterpart, Dr. S. Jaishankar, for the Australia-India Foreign Ministers' Framework Dialogue. The Quad partnership is vital for a peaceful Indo-Pacific. Ministers will discuss maritime security, critical minerals, and infrastructure development.

items.summary: The capacity of policymakers to respond to shocks is down across the board

items.title: Who was Toshifumi Suzuki? All about the founder of Japan’s iconic Seven‑Eleven stores who died at 93
items.summary: Toshifumi Suzuki, the former chairman of Seven & i Holdings and founder of Seven-Eleven Japan, has died at 93. Credited with transforming Japan’s retail industry, Suzuki introduced the convenience store franchise model in 1973 and helped build Seven-Eleven into a global retail giant.

items.title: RBI ready to do 'whatever is required' to curb rupee volatility: Sanjay Malhotra
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is ready to act to stabilize the foreign exchange market. Governor Sanjay Malhotra indicated the rupee may be undervalued. The central bank has tools and reserves to manage volatility. Inflation remains the primary focus for monetary policy. India's economic fundamentals are strong despite global pressures.

items.title: Huawei plans 1.4nm chips by 2031 as China races to catch TSMC
items.summary: Huawei is working to reduce its five-year semiconductor production gap with TSMC by producing 1.4-nanometer chips by 2031.

items.title: Oil drops below $100 on Iran peace hopes, but fuel prices rise again
items.summary: Brent crude fell over 4% below $100 a barrel on hopes of an Iran-US peace deal, but Indian fuel retailers raised petrol and diesel prices for the fourth time in two weeks.

items.title: CNBC Daily Open: Trump keeps the world guessing with mixed messaging on Iran deal
items.summary: While he says that Washington will not rush into a deal, energy stockpiles risk hitting the red zone this summer.

items.summary: Sanjay Malhotra speaks on RBI's priorities in inflation versus growth, the current account, forex market interventions, and the trajectory of  the rupee.

items.title: Flipkart bets games and AI can turn Bharat’s next internet users into shoppers
items.summary: Shopsy is being rebuilt around feeds, rewards and entertainment as Flipkart prioritizes digital habit formation over immediate purchases in India’s hyper-value e-commerce market.

items.title: India's solar export boom collapses as US tariffs bite
items.summary: All but one of India’s top listed solar module companies reported nil exports during the quarter ended 31 March. Market leader Waaree Energies Ltd, which had so far managed to protect its exports to the US, also reported fewer outbound shipments during the fourth quarter of FY26.

items.title: India's EV cell makers see first real shot at closing China price gap
items.summary: Lithium-ion cell makers are intensifying talks with the government to cut costs and build a local supplier base, as a 9% rise in imported Chinese cell prices offers a window to narrow the price gap. The push is urgent as EV cell imports hit $4.6 billion in FY26, over two-thirds of it from China.

items.title: 'I live in survival mode': The rise of the multi-job workforce
items.summary: More people are taking second jobs as rising costs and insecure work reshape how we earn a living.

items.title: Visa-backed PayMate ties unpaid employee dues to long-pending DigiAsia deal, funding
items.summary: PayMate, the Visa-backed B2B payments firm that once chased a ₹1,500-crore IPO, has been unable to pay full salaries to some staff for up to a year, linking repayment to the closure of a restructured deal with DigiAsia Corp.

items.title: AI promised cost savings, but Microsoft and Uber say it’s costing more than human workers
items.summary: Microsoft has cancelled most of its Claude Code licences and Uber burned through its 2026 AI budget in four months. New data reveals why enterprise AI costs are rising even as token prices fall.

items.title: Beer boom goes flat as breweries call last orders
items.summary: The UK's brewery scene is shrinking as pubs close, costs rise and drinking habits change.

items.title: RBI governor feels rupee may be undervalued after recent depreciation
items.summary: The rupee is not overvalued and may, in fact, be undervalued after its recent depreciation against the dollar, RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra says in an interview.

items.title: Top IT firms' H-1B visas slump 40%, TCS worst hit while Infosys gains
items.summary: H-1B visa approvals for India’s top IT services firms fell 40% this year to 11,041. As the Trump administration cracks down on work visas and green cards, IT firms are shifting towards more offshore work and local hiring in the US.

items.title: India’s Q4 GDP growth seen at 7.3% amid Iran war tensions
items.summary: India's economy finished fiscal year 2026 strongly. Growth in the March quarter was boosted by domestic demand, agriculture, and services. Economists predict 7.3% GDP growth for the quarter. Full-year growth is projected at 7.6%. Higher crude prices may impact the current fiscal year. Official data releases on June 5.

items.title: Morocco wants tourists to visit Western Sahara. Some say it's tightening its control
items.summary: The Moroccan government wants more Western holidaymakers to visit the territory it claims to own.

items.title: Indian billionaires buy foreign companies as growth slows at home
items.summary: India Inc spent $18bn on global buyouts in 2025 and the deal value could cross $15bn in the first half of 2026.

items.title: Trump not rushing Iran deal, whacks critics as 'losers'
items.summary: The president said the U.S. naval blockade of Iranian ports would remain in effect until "an agreement is reached, certified, and signed."

items.title: Delivery cost increased by about 19% in Asia Pacific: Report
items.summary: Delivery costs in India and the Asia Pacific region saw a significant rise of nearly 19 percent year-on-year. This increase occurred between March and May. Customers in India are now showing a preference for reliable delivery schedules rather than just speed. Many are willing to pay extra for convenient delivery options.

items.title: ECB Convenes Banks to Fix Flaws Exposed by AI Models, FT Says
items.summary: The European Central Bank plans to press lenders to accelerate efforts to secure their IT systems after organizing a meeting on cybersecurity risks with the latest artificial intelligence models, the Financial Times reported.

items.summary: Disney's "The Mandalorian and Grogu" tallied and estimated $82 million in domestic ticket sales through its first three days in theaters.

items.summary: International graduates say a weak hiring market and changing immigration rules make it harder for them to achieve their American dream of working in the U.S.

items.title: 'Shameful' more spent on benefits than jobs for young people, says Milburn
items.summary: Reforms are needed of the welfare system to tackle the high numbers of young people not in work or education, says Alan Milburn.

items.title: As smartwatch fears fade, Ethos expands deeper into luxury watches
items.summary: Ethos says analogue watches are regaining relevance as aspiration-led purchases, prompting the retailer to expand stores, target younger buyers and push into luxury lifestyle categories despite margin pressure.

items.summary: It is so expensive to live in Cambridge a charity says people with jobs need subsidised food.
